proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with treatment_enzalutamide=0.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_abiraterone=0.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_docetaxel=0.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_olaparib=0.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_lu177_psma=0.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with mcrpc=0.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
Patients with msi_high=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with msi_high=0.
Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with psma_high=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with visceral_mets=0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower objective_response rate.

analyses.code: chi-square crosstab of treatment_enzalutamide vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide-treated RR=0.361 (n=20076) vs untreated RR=0.159 (n=29924), diff=+0.202, p<1e-300.
Abiraterone-treated RR=0.238 vs 0.241, diff=-0.003, p=0.47. No main effect.
Docetaxel-treated RR=0.240 vs 0.240, diff=0.000, p=0.91. No main effect.
Olaparib-treated RR=0.239 vs 0.240, diff=-0.001, p=0.89. No main effect.
Lu177-PSMA-treated RR=0.243 vs 0.240, diff=+0.003, p=0.60. No main effect.
Pembrolizumab-treated RR=0.239 vs 0.240, diff=-0.002, p=0.88. No main effect.
mCRPC=1 RR=0.154 vs mCRPC=0 RR=0.346, diff=-0.192, p<1e-300. Strong negative prognostic effect.
PSMA-high=1 RR=0.239 vs 0 RR=0.243, diff=-0.004, p=0.31. No effect.
Visceral mets RR=0.240 vs no visceral RR=0.240, diff=-0.001, p=0.87. No main effect.
ecog_ps=0 RR=0.282, =1 RR=0.228, =2 RR=0.181. Monotonic decrease with worse PS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_olaparib increases objective_response more in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in patients with brca2_mutation=0 (canonical PARP-inhibitor pairing).
Treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response more in patients with psma_high=1 than in patients with psma_high=0 (canonical PSMA-targeted radioligand pairing).
Treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response more in patients with msi_high=1 than in patients with msi_high=0 (canonical immune-checkpoint MSI pairing).
Treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response in ar_v7_positive=0 patients but provides no benefit (or reduced benefit) in ar_v7_positive=1 patients (AR-V7 resistance).
Treatment_abiraterone raises objective_response more in ar_v7_positive=0 patients than in ar_v7_positive=1 patients (AR-V7 resistance).

analyses.result_summary: In BRCA2=1: olaparib RR=0.121 (n=529) vs no-olaparib RR=0.153 (n=4467), diff=-0.032, subgroup p=0.058. Interaction coef=-0.289 (p=0.045) — direction OPPOSITE to canonical hypothesis. Not supported.
In PSMA-high=1: lu177 RR=0.238 vs no-lu177 RR=0.239, diff=-0.001 (p=0.88). Interaction coef=-0.054, p=0.36. No benefit.

analyses.code: logit objective_response ~ pembro*msi_high; crosstab in MSI-high
analyses.result_summary: In MSI-high=1: pembro RR=0.177 (n=79) vs no-pembro RR=0.176 (n=1449), diff=+0.001. Interaction coef=+0.017, p=0.96. No benefit.

In AR-V7=0: enz RR=0.411 (n=16057) vs no RR=0.160, diff=+0.251 (p<1e-300). In AR-V7+: enz RR=0.165 (n=4019) vs no RR=0.157, diff=+0.008 (p=0.27). Interaction coef=-1.237, p=1.4e-93. Strongly supported.

AR-V7=0: abi RR=0.257 vs no=0.262, diff=-0.005. AR-V7+: abi RR=0.163 vs no=0.158, diff=+0.005. Interaction coef=+0.063, p=0.33. Not supported — abiraterone has no main or AR-V7-modified effect on response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response in mcrpc=0 patients but provides no benefit in mcrpc=1 patients (castration-resistance modifies effect).
Treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response in brca2_mutation=0 patients but provides no benefit in brca2_mutation=1 patients.
Treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response in msi_high=0 patients but provides no benefit in msi_high=1 patients.

At least one of the six treatments has a significant interaction with at least one of the six categorical biomarkers (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high, psma_high, visceral_mets) on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef=-1.978, p<1e-300. In mcrpc=0 patients enz adds large response benefit; in mcrpc=1 it does not.
Interaction coef=-1.138, p=3.3e-42. In BRCA2 wild-type patients enz works; in BRCA2 mutated it does not.
Interaction coef=-1.283, p=1.8e-19. In MSI-low patients enz works; in MSI-high it does not.

analyses.code: Loop logit fits across 6 treatments x 6 categorical features (36 interactions)
analyses.result_summary: Significant interactions are dominated by enzalutamide x (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high). Other treatments yield only weak/inconsistent interactions: olaparib*brca2 (coef=-0.29 p=0.045, opposite to canonical), lu177*visceral_mets (coef=-0.19 p=0.010), abiraterone*brca2 (coef=+0.19 p=0.031). None replicate canonical pairings; no other treatment has a strong subgroup signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_enzalutamide has a large positive effect on objective_response in patients jointly satisfying mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, and essentially no effect in any patient violating one or more of these conditions.

Each of mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, msi_high=1 individually abolishes the enzalutamide treatment effect when other modifiers are favorable (i.e., the four predicates act conjunctively).

analyses.code: Crosstab enz x outcome stratified by enz_fav (=mcrpc==0 & arv7==0 & brca2==0 & msi==0)
analyses.result_summary: Favorable subgroup (N=15681, 31.4% of cohort): enz RR=0.798 vs no-enz RR=0.172, diff=+0.626 (p<1e-300). Any-unfavorable subgroup (N=34319): enz RR=0.161 vs no-enz RR=0.153, diff=+0.008 (p=0.057). The enz benefit is essentially confined to the favorable cell.

analyses.code: For each of the 4 modifiers, hold the other 3 favorable and stratify by the focal modifier (single-unfavorable cells)
analyses.result_summary: Holding the other three favorable: mcrpc=1 only -> enz diff=+0.007 (p=0.17); ar_v7=1 only -> diff=+0.021 (p=0.086); brca2=1 only -> diff=+0.004 (p=0.88); msi=1 only -> diff=-0.025 (p=0.58). Each modifier individually abolishes the effect; conjunctive structure confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within mcrpc=1 patients, at least one of the non-enzalutamide treatments (abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, pembrolizumab) raises objective_response.
Treatment_olaparib raises objective_response in mcrpc=1 AND brca2_mutation=1 patients (canonical PARP-inhibitor indication).
Treatment_lu177_psma raises objective_response in mcrpc=1 AND psma_high=1 patients (canonical Lu-177 indication).

analyses.code: Within df[mcrpc==1], chi-square each non-enz treatment vs response
analyses.result_summary: Within mCRPC: abi diff=+0.001 p=0.89, doc diff=-0.001 p=0.77, ola diff=+0.002 p=0.78, lu177 diff=+0.005 p=0.38, pembro diff=+0.006 p=0.59. None significant.
N=2724: olaparib RR=0.111 (n=296) vs no-olaparib RR=0.152 (n=2428), diff=-0.041, p=0.074. Direction OPPOSITE to canonical PARP indication; not significant.
N=16405: lu177 RR=0.159 vs no-lu177 RR=0.150, diff=+0.009, p=0.25. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher nlr is associated with lower objective_response.
age_years is associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Coef=-0.0047, p=1.6e-94. Higher PSA strongly associated with lower response.
Coef=+0.096, p=4.4e-6. Higher albumin associated with higher response.
Coef=-0.0069, p=5.6e-8. Higher CRP associated with lower response.
Coef=-0.032, p=3.4e-29. More weight loss associated with lower response.
Coef=-0.0001, p=0.68. No effect.
Coef=-0.0026, p=0.61. No effect.
Coef=+0.0002, p=0.82. No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for all features, mcrpc, ecog_ps, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, albumin_g_dl, and psa_ng_ml remain independent predictors of objective_response, while non-enzalutamide treatments and most labs do not.

analyses.code: logit obj_resp ~ all features + all 6 treatments
analyses.result_summary: Significant adjusted predictors (sign and p): mcrpc -1.13 (p<1e-300), ecog_ps -0.33 (<1e-80), brca2 -0.74 (1e-63), arv7 -0.69 (3e-108), msi_high -0.43 (3e-9), psa_ng_ml -0.001 (3e-8), albumin +0.13 (2e-8), weight_loss -0.036 (<1e-32), crp -0.007 (1e-7), enzalutamide +1.20 (<1e-300). Other 5 treatments and visceral_mets, gleason_score, psma_high, age, ldh, nlr, hb, ALP, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium all p>0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 6 treatments, at least one shows a significant interaction with at least one continuous feature (PSA, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, hemoglobin, ALP, age) on objective_response.

analyses.code: Loop logit fits across 6 treatments x 9 continuous features (54 interactions)
analyses.result_summary: Only nominally significant interaction: enzalutamide x psa_ng_ml (coef=-0.009, p=1e-72). All others p>0.05. The enz x PSA result reflects the favorable-subgroup gating (PSA correlates with mCRPC) rather than a separate continuous modifier — within the enz-favorable subgroup, the PSA x enz interaction p=0.79.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the enz-favorable subgroup (mcrpc=0 & ar_v7_positive=0 & brca2_mutation=0 & msi_high=0), the enzalutamide treatment effect remains large and approximately constant across psa_ng_ml deciles, visceral_mets status, ECOG, and gleason_score.

analyses.code: Within sub_fav, logit enz*psa; stratify by visceral_mets, ecog_ps, gleason_score, psa decile
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup: enz x psa interaction p=0.79; visceral_mets=0/1 both yield enz diff +0.626/+0.623; ECOG 0/1/2 yield enz diff +0.65/+0.62/+0.59; Gleason 6-10 yield enz diff +0.62-0.63; PSA deciles all show enz diff +0.59-0.65. Effect is essentially constant across these features within the favorable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner (gradient boosting) trained separately on each treated arm will identify the same 4 modifiers (mcrpc, ar_v7, brca2, msi) as the dominant drivers of enzalutamide treatment-effect heterogeneity, and will not identify a coherent subgroup with meaningful uplift for any other treatment.

analyses.code: Fit GradientBoostingClassifier on tx==1 and tx==0 separately; uplift = p1-p0; rank features via DecisionTreeRegressor on uplift
analyses.result_summary: For enzalutamide, top decile of predicted uplift shows actual diff +0.737 vs bottom decile -0.159; top features are mcrpc (0.61), ar_v7_positive (0.25), brca2_mutation (0.14). For abiraterone/docetaxel/olaparib/lu177/pembro, the apparent top-decile diffs (+0.27 to +0.60) and bottom-decile diffs (-0.24 to -0.36) are symmetric and trace to baseline-prognosis differences (mCRPC, albumin, ALP) rather than a true treatment-responsive subgroup. Confirmed in iterations 12 and 16.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab raises objective_response in msi_high=1, mcrpc=0 patients (a more clinically intuitive subset).
Within the enz-favorable subgroup, at least one of the 5 non-enzalutamide treatments raises objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: N=689: pembro RR=0.294 (n=34) vs no-pembro RR=0.194 (n=655), diff=+0.100. Suggestive but very small n_tx and not significant after multiplicity (95% CI crosses 0).

analyses.code: Within enz-favorable subgroup (N=15681), each non-enz treatment crosstab
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup: abi diff=-0.012, doc diff=+0.005, ola diff=-0.001, lu177 diff=-0.007, pembro diff=-0.017. None significant; if anything slightly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No treatment raises objective_response in mcrpc=1 patients, including the canonical pairings olaparib+BRCA2, lu177+PSMA-high, and pembrolizumab in mCRPC+AR-V7+ resistant disease.

analyses.code: Stratify mCRPC pts by biomarker x treatment
analyses.result_summary: mCRPC+BRCA2+: olaparib diff=-0.041, pembro diff=-0.009, lu177 diff=+0.021. mCRPC+PSMA-high: lu177 diff=+0.009, ola diff=+0.001, pembro diff=+0.016. mCRPC+AR-V7+: enz diff=+0.005, abi diff=+0.019, doc diff=+0.001, lu177 diff=-0.004, pembro diff=-0.043. All p>0.05; no canonical pairing reproduced.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Enzalutamide effect is only present when ALL FOUR favorable conditions hold; relaxing any single condition (with the other three favorable) returns enzalutamide effect to ~0.

analyses.code: Compute enz RR difference in each single-unfavorable cell with other 3 favorable
analyses.result_summary: All-favorable diff=+0.626 (p<1e-300); mcrpc=1-only diff=+0.007 (p=0.17); arv7=1-only diff=+0.021 (p=0.09); brca2=1-only diff=+0.004 (p=0.88); msi=1-only diff=-0.025 (p=0.58). Confirms conjunctive (AND) gating.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the enz-favorable subgroup, the enz absolute response benefit is approximately +0.6 across ECOG (0/1/2), visceral_mets (0/1), gleason (6-10), and psma_high (0/1), indicating no further multiplicative modifier.

analyses.code: Stratify favorable subgroup by each of ECOG, visceral_mets, gleason, psma_high; report enz RR diff
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0: +0.65; ECOG 1: +0.62; ECOG 2: +0.59. Visceral_mets 0/1: +0.626/+0.623. PSMA 0/1: +0.65/+0.61. Gleason 6/7/8/9/10: +0.63/+0.62/+0.64/+0.62/+0.63. Treatment effect is essentially constant at +0.60-0.65 across these features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the enz-favorable subgroup, enzalutamide effect does not depend on psma_high status.

analyses.code: Stratify favorable subgroup by psma_high; enz RR diff
analyses.result_summary: PSMA-high=0: enz RR=0.813 vs no=0.164, diff=+0.649. PSMA-high=1: enz RR=0.788 vs no=0.177, diff=+0.611. Essentially equivalent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among ALL 2-binary-feature subgroups (using mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2, ar_v7, msi_high, psma_high, high_psa, low_alb, high_crp, high_nlr, high_ldh, high_ecog), there exists a 2-feature subgroup in which abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, or pembrolizumab raises objective_response significantly (after considering multiplicity).

analyses.code: Loop over all 2-feature x 4-value combinations for each non-enz tx; rank by absolute RR diff
analyses.result_summary: Among hundreds of subgroups screened: top positive effects are abiraterone in BRCA2+, low-LDH (+0.035 p=0.024), pembrolizumab in BRCA2+ normal-albumin subset (+0.07 p=0.038), olaparib in 2-feature subsets (~+0.05 nominal). All effect sizes are small (<0.10), p-values do not survive any reasonable multiplicity correction, and signs are inconsistent across overlapping subgroups. Hypothesis not supported beyond chance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A single composite indicator enz_fav := (mcrpc=0 & ar_v7=0 & brca2=0 & msi=0) explains the heterogeneity of enzalutamide effect on objective_response with a strong positive interaction term in a covariate-adjusted logit model.

analyses.code: logit obj_resp ~ enz*enz_fav + ecog + visc + alb + crp + wt_loss
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted enz x enz_fav interaction coef=+2.94 (SE 0.052, p<1e-300). Main enz coef in non-favorable patients = +0.06 (small, marginally p=0.05). Composite favorable indicator captures the heterogeneity in one term.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ECOG, mCRPC, visceral mets, PSA, BRCA2, AR-V7, MSI-high, PSMA-high, albumin, weight loss, CRP, and concurrent enzalutamide, none of abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177-psma, or pembrolizumab has a significant effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: logit obj_resp ~ each_tx + (full covariate set) + treatment_enzalutamide
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effects: abiraterone -0.025 (p=0.31), docetaxel +0.002 (p=0.94), olaparib +0.025 (p=0.51), lu177 +0.029 (p=0.36), pembrolizumab +0.014 (p=0.79). All p>0.30. Confirmed: none of the five other treatments has a measurable effect on response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, the canonical biomarker pairings olaparib*brca2_mutation, lu177*psma_high, and pembrolizumab*msi_high have positive interaction coefficients on objective_response.

analyses.code: logit obj_resp ~ tx*biomarker + full covariate set
analyses.result_summary: olaparib*brca2 coef=-0.294 (p=0.051; opposite to canonical sign). lu177*psma_high coef=-0.047 (p=0.46). pembro*msi_high coef=-0.060 (p=0.85). None of the canonical pairings show the expected positive interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment assignment is approximately independent of all baseline biomarkers (mcrpc, ar_v7, brca2, msi, psma_high, visceral_mets), implying that the absence of treatment effects (and the presence of the enzalutamide HTE) is not due to confounding by indication.

analyses.code: chi-square: each treatment vs each binary feature
analyses.result_summary: All 36 treatment-by-feature chi-square p-values are >0.02, with most >0.20. Treatment-arm rates differ by at most 0.01-0.02 across feature levels. Treatment assignment is essentially random with respect to features; observed treatment-effect heterogeneity reflects real effect modification, not selection.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding all 4 enzalutamide x modifier interactions plus the 5 non-enz treatment main effects to a covariate-adjusted logit model: each enzalutamide x modifier interaction coefficient is large negative (~-1.5 to -2.2) and highly significant; main effects of the other 5 treatments remain non-significant.

analyses.code: logit obj_resp ~ enz*(mcrpc + arv7 + brca2 + msi) + ecog + visc + alb + crp + wt_loss + 5 other tx
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted enz interactions: enz*mcrpc=-2.23 (p<1e-300), enz*arv7=-1.64 (p=1e-138), enz*brca2=-1.55 (p=1e-66), enz*msi=-1.67 (p=1e-27). Main enz coef=+2.62. Other 5 treatments p>0.30. Within unfavorable subgroups, observed enz absolute differences: mCRPC +0.008 (p=0.09), AR-V7+ +0.008 (p=0.27), BRCA2+ +0.006 (p=0.57), MSI-high -0.022 (p=0.30).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the enz-favorable subgroup, ECOG, albumin, CRP, weight loss, and PSA remain independent prognostic predictors of objective_response (with enzalutamide as the dominant predictor), while LDH, NLR, visceral_mets, and PSMA-high are not.

analyses.code: Within enz-favorable, logit obj_resp ~ enz + ecog + alb + crp + wt_loss + psa + ldh + nlr + visceral + psma
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable: enz +3.02 (p<1e-300), ECOG -0.40 (p=4e-38), albumin +0.17 (p=6e-5), CRP -0.011 (p=2.5e-6), weight_loss -0.045 (p=2e-16), PSA -0.0057 (p=5e-5). LDH p=0.82, NLR p=0.46, visceral_mets p=0.89, PSMA p=0.73 — not independent predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists a synergistic two-treatment combination interaction on objective_response (e.g., enz+doc, abi+ola).

analyses.code: Loop logit obj_resp ~ tx_i * tx_j across all 15 treatment pairs
analyses.result_summary: Only nominally significant pair: enzalutamide x docetaxel coef=-0.106 (p=0.023, antagonistic). abiraterone x olaparib coef=-0.130 (p=0.092). Both are weak, the enz x doc result is dwarfed by the +2.6 enz main effect, and after multiplicity (15 tests) neither survives. No clear synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among ALL 3-binary-feature subgroups (8 binary indicators, all 2^3 value combinations), there exists a subgroup in which a non-enzalutamide treatment raises objective_response with effect size >+0.10 and chance-corrected significance.

analyses.code: Iterate all 3-feature triples and 8 value tuples; rank by RR difference
analyses.result_summary: Largest positive effects: pembro in (visceral_mets=1, brca2=1, low_alb=0) +0.167 N=514 p=0.029; lu177 in (visceral=0, arv7=1, msi=1) +0.148 N=240 p=0.065; pembro in (brca2=1, psma=0, low_alb=0) +0.123 N=1094 p=0.025. All have small absolute n_tx (30-55), p-values do not survive Bonferroni for the >1000 tested subgroups, and the broader BRCA2+ pembro effect is +0.011 (p=0.69). Hypothesis not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: Treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response by approximately +0.6 absolute (RR ~0.80 vs ~0.17) in patients jointly satisfying mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in any patient in whom one or more of mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, msi_high is positive, treatment_enzalutamide has no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response (point estimate <+0.025 in absolute terms).

FINAL: None of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, or treatment_pembrolizumab has a detectable causal effect on objective_response in this cohort, in the overall population or in any clinically motivated biomarker subgroup (BRCA2+ for olaparib, PSMA-high for lu177, MSI-high for pembrolizumab, AR-V7+ for abiraterone).

Sensitivity check: the nominal pembrolizumab signal in the (BRCA2+, high-albumin, visceral_mets+) subgroup does not generalize — within all BRCA2+ patients, pembrolizumab effect is +0.011 (p=0.69), and after adjustment p=0.74.

FINAL: Adverse prognostic factors for objective_response (independent of treatment) are mcrpc=1, ecog_ps high, brca2_mutation=1, ar_v7_positive=1, msi_high=1, low albumin_g_dl, high crp_mg_l, high weight_loss_pct_6mo, and high psa_ng_ml. visceral_mets, gleason_score, psma_high, age_years, ldh_u_l, nlr, hemoglobin, ALP, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, and calcium are NOT independent predictors after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Whole BRCA2+: pembro RR=0.161 (n=249) vs no-pembro RR=0.149 (n=4747), diff=+0.011, p=0.69. Adjusted in BRCA2+: pembro coef=+0.058, p=0.74. Three-feature signal (effect +0.167 in N=514) does not generalize.

Favorable (mcrpc=0 & arv7=0 & brca2=0 & msi=0; N=15681): enz RR=0.798 (n=6325) vs no-enz RR=0.172 (n=9356), abs diff=+0.626, p<1e-300. Any-unfavorable (N=34319): enz RR=0.161 vs no-enz RR=0.153, abs diff=+0.008, p=0.057. Enzalutamide effect is concentrated in the conjunctive favorable subgroup.

Adjusted main effects (p): abi -0.025 (0.36), doc +0.011 (0.66), ola +0.015 (0.71), lu177 +0.033 (0.31), pembro +0.050 (0.36). No subgroup-restricted positive effect for any non-enz treatment survives multiplicity correction in main biomarker pairings, in mCRPC, in 2-feature, or in 3-feature subgroup searches.

Adjusted significant (sign, p): mcrpc -1.13 (1e-300), ecog_ps -0.33 (1e-82), brca2 -0.74 (1e-63), arv7 -0.69 (3e-108), msi_high -0.43 (3e-9), psa -0.001 (3e-8), albumin +0.13 (2e-8), weight_loss -0.036 (1e-32), crp -0.007 (1e-7). Non-significant after adjustment: visceral_mets, gleason_score, psma_high, age_years, ldh, nlr, hemoglobin, ALP, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium.
